title: Is AI actually this generation's internet boom, or are we overhyping it?
author: thienpro2
selftext:
Looking back at the 90s dot-com era or the early 2010s mobile shift, the biggest winners were always the ones who positioned before the tech became utility infrastructure. Right now, AI feels like it's reaching that exact same foundational layer status. 

Most retail investors only notice the trend after mega-caps like Nvidia go parabolic. But historically, the real asymmetry happens much earlier, during the pre-IPO or early adoption phases. I noticed platforms like BingX are starting to experiment with pre-IPO AI exposure and tech-focused airdrops recently, which is an interesting shift away from just standard speculative tokens. 

How are you guys actually positioning for the long-term AI shift?

comments.author: HelloGoodbye2894
comments.body: These kind of projects made by the said platform is  very useful to users to so we can join as early as we can before it's too late.

comments.author: Minimum_Raccoon_1501
comments.body: It’s probably bigger.  Internet was intercommunication, social connection, more stuff, more things.  This is a much deeper change.

comments.author: chicadepanem
comments.body: It can't be hype with all the advances we're making in AI

comments.author: CoolVermicelli8349
comments.body:
Ai is a scam - hundreds of billions just so we can search faster, write better emails and make coding more cumbersome to debug
Only use case that is legitimate is in call centers and there too i rather talk to a human

comments.author: eggrally
comments.body: ai data center moving to space

comments.author: eggrally
comments.body: hype for a couple months, then on to the next new thing

comments.author: flashamazin
comments.body: I think the biggest edge right now is focusing on infrastructure rather than hype — chips, compute, data, and AI tooling. Most people chase narratives late, but the long-term winners are usually the ones building the rails everyone else depends on.

comments.author: otetmarkets
comments.body: Feels like the internet era all over again: most “AI projects” will wash out, but the winners will be the ones with real distribution and a clear use case, not just a model wrapper. The edge is in data, workflow, and switching costs once the models start looking interchangeable.
